column2: A required application is 64-bit, but the target computer runs a 32-bit operating system. Can the application be installed as a compatible solution?
column4: No. A 64-bit application requires a 64-bit operating system.
column5: The reverse relationship is different: 64-bit Windows can commonly run 32-bit applications. Always compare the application's architecture requirement with the installed OS.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.10; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 22
column10: A+::220-1202::1.10 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::ApplicationInstallationRequirements Basic HighYield Applications Compatibility Architecture Source::Messer-v140

column2: An application meets the workstation's CPU and RAM requirements, but its publisher does not list the installed operating system as supported. What requirement still blocks the installation decision?
column5: Adequate hardware does not establish software compatibility. Confirm the supported OS and version before treating the system as ready.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.10; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 22
column10: A+::220-1202::1.10 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::ApplicationInstallationRequirements Basic Applications Compatibility Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: A 3D application requires a dedicated graphics card, but a laptop has only integrated graphics. What requirement is unmet?
column5: With integrated graphics, the CPU and GPU are on the same chip, and graphics uses system memory. Dedicated, or discrete, graphics uses a separate graphics card with its own video memory.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.10; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 22
column10: A+::220-1202::1.10 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::ApplicationInstallationRequirements Basic HighYield Applications Graphics Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: Before installing a graphics-intensive application, which graphics-memory specification should a technician compare with the application's requirement?
column5: VRAM is video random-access memory used by a dedicated graphics card. Do not substitute the computer's total system RAM for a stated VRAM requirement.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.10; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 22
column10: A+::220-1202::1.10 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::ApplicationInstallationRequirements Basic Applications Graphics VRAM Source::Messer-v140

column2: A workstation has exactly the RAM listed for a new application, but the OS and several required applications will run at the same time. What should the technician consider before installation?
column4: Whether enough RAM remains for the new application after the OS and concurrent applications use memory.
column5: An application's RAM requirement is above the memory already needed by the OS. Insufficient available RAM can cause poor performance or prevent the application from operating.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.10; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 22
column10: A+::220-1202::1.10 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::ApplicationInstallationRequirements Basic HighYield Applications Memory Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: What should a technician compare when checking whether a workstation meets an application's CPU requirement?
column4: Compare the installed processor and its processing speed with the application's published CPU requirement.
column5: CPU demand varies by workload. Use the application's stated requirement rather than assuming that any processor with a high clock number is compatible.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.10; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 22
column10: A+::220-1202::1.10 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::ApplicationInstallationRequirements Basic Applications CPU Requirements Source::Messer-v140

column2: A specialized application installs successfully but will not operate unless a particular USB device is connected. What requirement is this device satisfying?
column5: The token authorizes application use. It must remain available to the assigned user or system, so token management affects ongoing operation as well as the initial installation.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.10; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 22
column10: A+::220-1202::1.10 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::ApplicationInstallationRequirements Basic Applications Licensing HardwareToken Source::Messer-v140

column2: An installer fits in the available drive space, but the application will create large project files after installation. Which storage requirement should drive the readiness decision?
column4: The total space needed for the installed application and its ongoing use, not just the installer size.
column5: Installation media size, installed size, and working-data growth are different. The destination needs enough capacity for the application's practical use.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.10; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 22
column10: A+::220-1202::1.10 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::ApplicationInstallationRequirements Basic HighYield Applications Storage Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician receives an optical-disc image as one file and the workstation has no optical drive. How can the technician expose the installation files to the operating system?
column4: Mount the ISO file so it appears as a separate drive.
column5: An ISO preserves the contents of optical media in a file. Mounting uses the image directly; physical media would instead require a compatible drive or port.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.10; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 22
column10: A+::220-1202::1.10 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::ApplicationInstallationRequirements Basic Applications Distribution ISO Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician will use a downloadable application package. Which source should be preferred over an unrelated third-party download site?
column4: The software publisher's official download or a trusted centralized application store.
column5: The distribution method is electronic, but the source still matters. An unrelated third-party site adds uncertainty that a direct publisher source or centralized application store avoids.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.10; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 22
column10: A+::220-1202::1.10 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::ApplicationInstallationRequirements Basic Applications Distribution Download Source::Messer-v140

column2: An organization needs many matching virtual machines with the OS and required applications already installed. Which distribution method best fits this goal?
column5: A prepared image installs a complete, consistent system build quickly. It must match the target platform; virtual machines simplify this because their presented hardware can be identical.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.10; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, pp. 22-23
column10: A+::220-1202::1.10 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::ApplicationInstallationRequirements Basic HighYield Applications Distribution Imaging Source::Messer-v140

column2: After an application upgrade, one workstation becomes slow and some local files are missing. Which installation-impact category do these effects represent?
column5: These effects occur on the endpoint itself. Network access, workflow changes, and organization-wide downtime belong to separate impact categories.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.10; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 23
column10: A+::220-1202::1.10 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::ApplicationInstallationRequirements Basic Applications Impact Device Source::Messer-v140

column2: A newly installed application must reach an internal service and write to a shared folder. Which impact category requires review?
column4: Network impact, including service access and permissions to the share.
column5: Successful local installation does not prove that network dependencies are available. Required access and share permissions must support the application's use.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.10; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 23
column10: A+::220-1202::1.10 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::ApplicationInstallationRequirements Basic HighYield Applications Impact Network Source::Messer-v140

column2: An application update changes the steps employees must follow for a time-sensitive daily task. Which installation-impact category is this?
column5: Operational impact concerns how work is performed. Even a functioning application can disrupt a workflow when its process changes.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.10; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 23
column10: A+::220-1202::1.10 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::ApplicationInstallationRequirements Basic Applications Impact Operations Source::Messer-v140

column2: Downtime during an application change would stop a critical process and delay other departments that depend on its results. Which impact category is this?
column5: Business impact captures consequences to critical processes and downstream teams. It is broader than a single device problem or a changed user workflow.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.10; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 23
column10: A+::220-1202::1.10 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::ApplicationInstallationRequirements Basic Applications Impact Business Source::Messer-v140
